payload.summary: All four amendments identify genuine, verifiable issues in the codebase. Amendment fr-13-d40509ba targets a silent contract violation that loses diagnostic information. Amendment fr-13-ebee7dc9 proposes a test guard against the same drift-prone merge pattern already tested elsewhere. Amendment fr-13-ad662561 identifies a documented-but-unfixed race condition in plan_hash auto-population. Amendment fr-13-a84fe7e0 identifies a semantic mismatch in None handling between model documentation and adapter logic that can corrupt legacy entries. I accept all four after verifying each against the source code.

votes.rationale: Verified at fs.rs:2837-2838: when run_id is Some but started_at is None, the function silently returns None with no log or error. The surrounding comment (lines 2834-2836) acknowledges this is a contract enforcement check, yet the violation produces no diagnostic output. Callers eventually surface an error, but the specific reason (run_id without started_at) is lost, making production debugging unnecessarily difficult. Adding a tracing::warn! before the return is minimal effort and high diagnostic value. The amendment is correct and actionable.

Verified at model.rs:320-342: merge_attempt_entries manually copies run_id, plan_hash, snapshot_plan_hash_at_creation, outcome_detail, started_at, and finished_at. This is the exact same drift-prone pattern that journal details already guard against with the test at fs.rs:3922 (journal_merge_fills_all_optional_fields). A new optional field added to TaskRunEntry without a corresponding merge line would silently drop data. A round-trip coverage test following the established pattern is cheap insurance against this regression class. The amendment is well-motivated and consistent with existing testing patterns.

Verified at fs.rs:3034-3055: the code auto-populates plan_hash from the current snapshot hash via `or_else(|| current_snapshot_hash.clone())` at line 3046. The inline comment at lines 3037-3042 explicitly documents the race: persist_plan() between dispatch and record_task_run_start stamps the entry with the wrong plan version. The comment itself recommends callers always supply plan_hash explicitly, confirming the fallback path is known-unsafe. Documenting a race does not fix it — the auto-population still produces permanently incorrect lineage. The amendment correctly identifies this as an unresolved data corruption path.

Verified the semantic mismatch: model.rs:286-290 documents snapshot_plan_hash_at_creation=None as 'legacy entries that predate this field,' but fs.rs:2608-2621 treats None as 'entry was created before any plan was persisted' and backfills the current snapshot hash into both snapshot_plan_hash_at_creation and plan_hash. These interpretations are not equivalent. A legacy entry created under plan V1 (before the field was added) will have None, and when the adapter encounters it after the plan advances to V2, it will stamp V2 onto the entry — silently corrupting lineage for exactly the legacy rows the provenance guard was designed to protect. The amendment correctly identifies that the None case needs disambiguation (e.g., sentinel value or migration flag) to distinguish 'field did not exist yet' from 'no plan existed at creation time.'
